stem: A cloud computing server cluster uses 4 parallel nodes for redundancy. The request served requires at least one node to function. node 1 has a reliability of 79%. node 2 has a reliability of 89%. node 3 has a reliability of 90%. node 4 has a reliability between 76% and 91% (uncertain due to limited operational data). Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all nodes simultaneously, estimated between 3.8% and 8.4%. What is the overall probability that the request served?

ground_truth.answer: P(request served) ranges from 0.9155 to 0.9618. Point estimate: 0.9386.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 4 nodes fail simultaneously).
With common-cause failure (3.8%-8.4%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9155, 0.9618].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
